explanation: This command uses environment variables and string substitution to access the history file. It avoids direct string concatenation and uses standard PowerShell syntax to bypass simple string-based detection.
This command uses shorthand aliases (gc for Get-Content, sls for Select-String) and environment variables to obfuscate the command slightly while maintaining the same functionality.
This command uses the Join-Path cmdlet to construct the file path dynamically, making it more difficult for simple string-based detection rules to identify.
This command replaces Select-String with Where-Object and a regex match, changing the syntax while preserving the same behavior, which may evade pattern-based detection.
